content.full_text:
When Lobbyist and Wife Arranges For Wedding Gift, the $10 Prohibition Becomes a $20 Prohibition.

September 26, 1977, Opinion Request meeting decided that the Torres opinion permits a lobbyist and his wife to give a $20 gift and not violate the $10 gift prohibition.
content.full_text_markdown:
When Lobbyist and Wife Arranges For Wedding Gift, the $10 Prohibition Becomes a $20 Prohibition.

September 26, 1977, Opinion Request meeting decided that the Torres opinion permits a lobbyist and his wife to give a $20 gift and not violate the $10 gift prohibition.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a lobbyist and spouse may jointly give a $20 wedding gift without violating the $10 gift prohibition under California law.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that a lobbyist and his wife may together give a $20 gift without violating the $10 gift prohibition, based on the Torres opinion.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; appears to be a summary of an opinion request meeting decision. Title and meeting date suggest this is a meeting decision summary rather than a full advice letter format. Limited context on Torres opinion reference.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a lobbyist and spouse may jointly give a $20 wedding gift without violating the $10 gift prohibition under California law.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC determined that a lobbyist and his wife may together give a $20 gift without violating the $10 gift prohibition, based on the Torres opinion.
embedding.first_500_words: When Lobbyist and Wife Arranges For Wedding Gift, the $10 Prohibition Becomes a $20 Prohibition. September 26, 1977, Opinion Request meeting decided that the Torres opinion permits a lobbyist and his wife to give a $20 gift and not violate the $10 gift prohibition.
embedding.summary: An FPPC advice letter determining that a lobbyist and spouse may jointly present a $20 wedding gift, with the $10 prohibition effectively doubled when both parties participate together.
